name: Elmore, Rosebur Alexander
motto: Think before you make a promise and then stick to it—a good character is rather to be chosen than great riches.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer, Lancaster County Club, '25
description: He achieved this position on his military merit after taking the whole military staff by storm and being on excellent terms with Captain Lee. These two have taken many a ride to Anderson for the purpose of seeing the fairer sex. He is interested in horticulture, being one of "George Peter's" ardent followers. Some of these days we all expect him to own a large orchard from which he may clean up a million. He was always given most of the extra work in the Section, as was implied in these words: "Mr. Alexander, will you do . . . ." He never shirked any of this work and we predict that all through life he will never mind the daily grind and will wind up as a great success. Go to it, "Alec," we are all with you. With the ability as a horticulturist, and that wonderful girl of yours, you can make oranges and peaches grow in the Arctic Regions.

name: Olin, Samuel Anderson
motto: Life does not consist in holding a good hand, but in playing a bad hand well.
description: Olin, better known by his admirers, both male and female, as "Hambone", or more intimately as just plain "Bone", achieved fame at Clemson just for his peculiarly accentuated speech and for his marvelous success with the "unfair" sex. "Bone's" manipulation of the ladies' hearts is not unlike Ponzi's manipulation of dollars. He soon decided that the world was greatly in need of a good mechanical engineer. One might think that Anderson, being an ardent disciple of a certain movie actor whose name reminds one of the most useful of petroleum by-products, is not a good student. This, however, is not the case. His record as a student has not been phenomenal, but he, like the rest of us, has waded through four years of classes and now must start his life as a graduate. It is impossible for anyone to believe that anything but success shall be Anderson's lot.

name: Caughman, James Bankston
motto: Better to be small and shine than great and cast a shadow.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer, Richland County Club
Sophomore, Junior and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: Here, Gentle Reader, we present Red Caughman, the man of many sides, of many honors, and of many love affairs. Right well has Red upheld the honor of a family rich in the Clemson tradition. His brainy head and engineering skill, as demonstrated in the classrooms of Clemson and the Highway Department of South Carolina, assure his professional success. His financial wizardry, as demonstrated in the handling of the business of the Tiger has kept him broke a smaller portion of the time than is the average Clemson boy. His grace as a follower of Terpsichore has won him fame in the ball-room. And girls from the icy, crystal waters of Lake Champlain to the muddy Seneca have raved about his hair and beautiful eyes. Some of the above, Kind Reader, is true; some is most unmitigated misstatement. But Red is Red, and Clemson proudly claims him as her own.

name: Chapman, William Ennis
motto: He who speaks ill of the mare will have to buy her.
clubs: Member, Beaux Arts Institute of Design
description: Big Bill, as we all know him, needs no introduction to us or to the ladies of Anderson College. He came to us from Denver, not in Colorado, but South Carolina, to study architecture. It seems as if Bill is making a place for himself, not only in Clemson Architecture, but of the world. He has already won a prize with one of his drawings, competing with about six hundred architects of high repute. Keep it up, old boy, we are all behind you. Bill with all his talents is a likeable chap; honest and respected by all who know him. Here's wishing you good luck!

name: Cobb, Charles Nathaniel
motto: "It is in general more profitable to reckon up our defects than to boast of our attainments."
clubs: Member, Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: In the fall of '21 Charlie came to Clemson from the prep school where he had been holding his own as ranking cadet officer in the military department, and here has prepared himself to conquer new worlds through his military ability. Since coming to Clemson, Charlie's fame grew and he soon acquired the name of being the most military man in the regiment. After three years he had to drop out of college, and when he returned all of his old classmates had left. This intermission was very unfortunate, for if he had gone on in his class there is little doubt but that he should have attained the highest ranking military office among the cadets. We are proud to have known him for he is a typical gentleman, and in addition is a man who possesses great personal qualities for friendship. Here's hoping that the future will hold nothing but success for you, C. N., for you have our heartiest wishes in anything that you may undertake.

name: Coleman, Henry Crim
motto: "Deep in the heart of every man there lurks a beast; and foolish is the woman who arouses it."
clubs: Member, Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: Henry Coleman, rare combination of energy, wit, business ability, and "affaires de coeur," may be briefly characterized in the following terms: scholar, gentleman, good fellow, and a true friend. Henry possesses the rare quality of being perfectly at home wherever he may be, and, like the proverbial Pullman porter, he has a girl in every town. Here's to you, Henry. We wish you all the success in the world. Impress the world as you have impressed us and you will have achieved success.

name: Corn, Horace Elmer
motto: Scorn not the man who is down today, for he may be up tomorrow.
description: Yes, this is an honorary member of Specks Smith's wind-jammers. He blew into Clemson four years ago from the Electric City for the purpose of becoming saturated with the knowledge of electricity, and since that time has been a permanent chaser of the electron. He is a most loyal Tiger. He has not flourished as a military genius nor as an athlete, but he is never missing when the Tigers go into action. Sincerity, good will, and cheerfulness are the things that characterize this lad. This boy is always willing to lend a helping hand to those who need it and his many friends wish him a life of success in this world of trials and temptations.

name: Cox, Herbert Alvin
motto: Be yourself.
clubs: Member Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: Alvin, better known as came to us from the very heart of the Gamecock's roost, but under the prevalence of the soph's brooms and the Tiger spirit, which dominates all the Piedmont region, he soon became a true Tiger. Puss numbers his friends by his acquaintances. His friendship not only includes all of the people of the campus, but extends to all the neighboring towns. He seldom fails to visit Anderson on the week-ends, and has not failed to impress the fairer sex of the Electric City. There is also a more serious side to than girls and dancing at which he is an expert, namely, his work. He is an aspiring Electrical Engineer, and some day we expect him to put Steinmetz in the background.

name: Cox, Samuel McCarvey
motto: He is a wise man who always knows what to do next.
clubs: Fourth Corps Area National Rifle Team at Camp Perry, Ohio
description: The prime constituent of McGarvey's nature is the ability to concentrate and analyze. He absorbs the intricate deviousness of electricity with ease where other hard-working students are baffled. Humor, sageness, and pleasure are the leading instruments in his life symphony that is devoid of discord. He accepts the most appealing parts of life and rejects the rest without a tremor of hesitation. His genius is inventive, and should lead him to realms of renown and to the foremost ranks of those who have contributed to man's comfort on this terrestrial ball.

name: Cudd, John Enoch
motto: Of all the heavenly gifts that mortal men command, What trusty treasure in the world can countervail a friend?
clubs: Vice-President, Secretary and Treasurer, Architectural Society, '24
Member, Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
Member, Beaux Arts Institute of Design
description: In the fall of '22, Enoch came to Clemson to prepare himself to make a living for two. During his first week at Clemson he drew so many old boys around his room with brooms that he decided to cast his lot with the "drawers." After four years of training, Enoch can draw anything from flies to the most stately of mansions. We, the Class of '26, wish you the best in life. Your ability as a worker and as a peacemaker will carry you far on your road to success. Here's to you, Enoch, you have never gone back on a friend.

name: Culler, Francis Newton
motto: Every man is like the company he is wont to keep.
description: Out of the little town of Swansea came "Inspiration" for the class of '26. Newt, as he is known by his friends and college mates, has been throughout his college career an earnest and faithful worker, and stands high in the scholastic world. Newt has made many friends at the various female colleges over the state, as well as at Clemson. So eager was he to secure the fundamental principles of wisdom that no time was lost either in his room or in his classrooms. With his winning smiles and unique expressions he left the Education Section full of pep and laughter as they travelled to and from Seneca on the "Tsukiyama Special." A man's college career without a doubt has been a success when he has proved himself a friend, a scholar, and a gentleman. Here's to you, Newt; peace, success, and happiness. The class of '26 will always cherish your memory.

name: Darby, James McDow
motto: Be wise with speed; A fool at forty is a fool indeed.
clubs: Member Junior and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: To his many friends he is affectionately known as Jim. This young man who hails from the metropolis called Sandy Springs, has for the past four years been winning the admiration and friendship of his fellow classmates. Jim is the type of man that makes friends. Although he has never suffered from nervous prostration due to overstudy, he has put forth his untiring efforts toward his objective—a diploma—and right well has he succeeded. Jim has one weakness, and that is for Anderson and "Her." Anyway, each week-end finds Jim in the nearby city, and the balmy atmosphere around this city seems to have a soothing effect upon his heart trouble. His pleasing personality has won for him a host of friends who desire him to succeed in all that he undertakes.

name: Davis, James Archie
motto: No man has done his duty until he has done his best.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Sumter County Club, '24
description: Girls beware! This lad has two hobbies: one is girls and the other is electricity. He came to Clemson in '22 from the Gamecock City to study Electricity, but in his four years he has learned almost as much about the power of the other sex as he has about electricity. Sonny, since becoming one of "Crip's" disciples, has learned a great deal about electricity—he can screw a light bulb into a socket every time. James is a very ambitious student. His ideals have been of the very highest. He is a gentleman at all times. We predict for him a very successful future. May he some day achieve his ambitions, and may all of his trouble be small ones.

name: Day, Charlie Brown
motto: Wisdom is knowing what to do next, skill is knowing how to do it, and virtue is doing it.
clubs: Corresponding Secretary, Senior Critic, President, Columbian Literary Society
Howitzer and Pistol Marksman, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: He left home to light up the world and he has proved his ability by mastering electricity. Charlie has shown himself a true friend to all who know him. He is not one of these loud and boisterous fellows, but on the contrary one who moves onward by his ever diligent work. His work in athletics has not been spectacular, but steady. Charlie's one great weakness is the fairer sex; whether he gets results or not we do not know, but something causes him to be absent from college every week-end. "Military" is Day's middle name. Not only has he been a leader in this branch in his college career, but as a soldier he can be counted on always to do his duty. The Class of '26 wishes to him great success in his future undertakings, and that he will make good is a matter of no speculation.

name: Douglass, William Joseph
motto: A square deal for every man.
description: Doug began his career with the class of '25; but letting his conscience be his guide he left us for a year. In '24 he returned to us, hoping to make Clemson "The West Point on the Seneca." Douglass, after a careful consideration, has cast his lot with the Military Department. Although the Army is his chief objective, he does excellent work in all of his classes. He always leaves his mark of good workmanship behind him. Whatever it may be, when he has something to do, he is about it. Yet he takes time out to remember the fairer sex at Winthrop. Doug, we hate to see you leave Clemson, and the sophomores will certainly miss you next year. Yet as you go away, the eyes of Clemson will be watching you for your success on your life work. By continuing to be square, Doug, you cannot help but rise.

name: Elliot, William Robert Jr.
motto: Do nothing on the sly.
description: Winnsboro, a stronghold of Clemson loyalty, is known by the class of '26 as the thriving town from which Bill Elliot came to Clemson. Bill is one of those quiet boys who do not blatantly force themselves upon one's attention, or other activities, but who diligently do their part, be it in the class-room, athletics, or what not, meanwhile making a host of friends. He has been an all-round college man. He was a faithful worker on the track and a tireless supporter of all branches of athletics. He has rendered invaluable service also as a contributor to the several publications of school. Bill's unflinching loyalty to high principles has made him a leader. The Class of '26 will long cherish the memory of this noble member.

name: Epting, James Claude
motto: If you want to, you can.
clubs: Rifle Marksman, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Epting was first a member of the class which welcomed us so warmly when we were rats. The class of '26 is very fortunate in having him join them. He was not very widely known when he appeared in Tigertown for his second scholastic campaign. Since then, however, he has won many true friends with his pleasing personality. In barracks Claude's favorite pastime is delving into books on the subject of agriculture. In his classes he works hard and consistently and is among the best men in his section. He is a follower of Lord (Collins, Ph.D.) and is determined to show the people of his community what he can do. Here's the best of luck to you, Epting, in whatever you may wish to undertake.

name: Ergle, David Ramsey
motto: Let him not belong to another who may be his own.
description: 'Tis true that Ramsey had to put clods in his shoes to feel at home, but how could we expect better when we consider the fact that he came from the "Cracker State." He decided to "cast his die" with the "test tube washers," and since then he has been trying to find the unknown. Not only does Ramsey excel in his class work, but he has also achieved much in the musical realms of Tigertown. He "blew" his way through the military department, ending as Band Captain. Besides this he has been a mainstay in both the Concert Orchestra and with the "Jungaleers." Ramsey is a cadet, a true Tiger, a real friend, and a gentleman. Remember, old boy, we are looking at you and are expecting nothing but the best from you, so luck to you!

name: Evans, James Key
motto: If you don't say a good word for your fellow man, do not speak at all.
description: This youth is a good student. He pursues his work with vigor and takes pride in solving difficult problems. It is seldom that we find such a combination of good qualities as Jim possesses, molded in the personality of one individual. He is a sincere friend, a lover of work and fun, a practical idealist, a respecter of himself and others, a good mixer, and a likeable chap. Possessing, as he does, so many rare good qualities, we predict for Jim a bright and sunny future. May you always be the same ole Jim, ever sparkling on the road to success. Luck to you, Jim, a true friend, a loyal Tiger, and a devoted pal.

name: Ezell, Paul Bryson
motto: Work as though you were going to live forever; live as though you were going to die tomorrow.
description: Ezell came to Clemson in the fall of '21 from the "city" of Cross Anchor, and here for the past few years he has been busy acquiring that elusive thing called higher learning. Due to unforeseen circumstances, however, P. B. was forced to drop out of the running for awhile and is, consequently, finishing with the Class of '26. During the war P. B. served his country overseas; now he is going to serve at home by teaching. Surely there is no finer task a man can turn his hand to than the training of the youth of the world, and it is in this great calling that Ezell has enlisted. Here's luck to you, Ezell; may life give you all that you so richly deserve in return for the unselfish attitude that you have towards it.

name: Farmer, William Anderson
motto: He who steals my purse steals trash; but he who steals my pipe steals my very heart.
description: "Bill" is consistently a hard worker for the things he believes to be worth while. That is why the class of '26 made him president on its last important lap of college. Soon after he entered Clemson, "Bud" helped to organize a bachelor's club. Did he remain in it? Not by a long shot, as the postmaster will tell you. Bud, as you mush through the icy fields of the trackless Arctic, hold still a warm place in your heart for the old class of '26, which after four years of association with you, has grown to know and admire you.

name: Fewell, Joel Alexander
motto: Never let pleasure interfere with work, but let your conscience be your guide.
clubs: Member Junior and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: "Jack," for thus he is known to everyone, has by his manliness won the admiration and respect of all his classmates. This lad hails from Rock Hill; and Rock Hill's loss was Clemson's gain. He is endowed with an attractive personality, and he has the ability to think and act for himself. Jack has taken part in many of the college activities, but his performances on the gridiron have been the means of earning for him the plaudits of all true Tigers. If he tackles the obstacles in his way to success as he tackled his opponents on the football field, he is sure to mount to the top in his chosen profession. He is determined to become one of the bright and shining lights of the Textile world, and he carries with him the best wishes of his many friends for his future success.

name: Bailey, Thomas Lafayette Walker Jr.
motto: He is a wise man who always knows what to do next.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Laurens County Club
description: Thomas Lafayette Walker Bailey, Jr. came to us from the metropolis of Clinton. He was quite a handsome rat and since that time has become a campus sheik. He has more girls in love with him than Rudolph Valentino(?). He is a disciple of "Big Ben." During his last year at school, he roomed with "Bill" Lippincott and this has given him an even greater reputation with the ladies. The ties that these boys wear are more than enough to win any girl's heart. Although he has not led his class as a student, his record is one of which he should be proud. After all, one's class record is only a small part of one's success. He has always been active in the various branches of student activities. He seems to realize that a college course means a course in the art of living with one's fellow-men. He is a natural-born gentleman and if his success in after life comes to him as easily as it has come here, there can be no obstacle too great for him to overcome.

name: Barr, Clyde Mitchell
motto: Thou wouldst be loved? Then let thy heart from its present pathway part not.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Wade Hampton Literary Society
Machine Gun Sharpshooter, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: If you were to meet this lad on the street you would probably think him a preacher, but he is not. His deceptive looks have earned for him the title of "Rev.", of which he is worthy in every respect. He came to Clemson in the fall of '22 and has been a consistent worker in the Civil Engineering Department since. Clemson should be proud of such a student as this lad because he is a conscientious worker and a friend to everybody. Barr is not only a conscientious worker but also a skilled writer to the "flavored" ones at ....... well, guess. We sincerely hope he will be as successful in winning friends among the ladies after he leaves Clemson as he has been among the boys at school. May the best in life be yours, old pal.

name: Finklea, Gary Ithemer
motto: Work without hope draws nectar in a sieve, And hope without an object cannot live.
clubs: Member Clemson R.O.T.C. Track Team, Camp McClellan, Ala.
First Class Machine Gunner, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Soon after Fink, as the big-hearted lad is affectionately known, came to Clemson he won our love and respect with his individuality, sincerity, and fineness of purpose. From the beginning of his prep-school days and on through college he has been recognized as a boy with great determination, and as one who has always stood up for what he thought best. Fink has won love and respect in all departments, and Crip can truly boast that he is one man who is worthy to serve as a model for all others, as the class of '26 is to us. Besides being a good student, Fink is a star in track and in football. He is undaunted by superior opposition and as captain of the football team, his indomitable courage and inspiring personality were assets of great value. The Class of '26 wishes you the greatest success which can be won in this world.

name: Fleming, John Melmoth
motto: Live your life as you see best, Play the game, Fate will do the rest.
description: Imagine a room in barracks—ten or twelve men gathered in one of the famous sessions—conversation flowing freely—and you have Mammoth's paradise. He has done his stuff for four years on the gridiron, playing the guard position like a veteran. He has made scores of friends who swear by him, and fortunate indeed is the man who can call him friend, for here he has one who will stick by him through thick and thin and never reckon the consequences. What other qualifications needs any man than these?

name: Foster, Rembert Melton
motto: Be not wise in thine own eyes; Fear the Lord, and depart from evil.
clubs: Recording Secretary, Corresponding Secretary, and President Palmetto Literary Society
description: To meet him is to like him, to know him is to love him. He has a heart that is big and true, a smile that would never wear off, and is a friend to both the boys and the ladies. He is a gentleman, an earnest scholar, a true friend, an exponent of the higher things of life, and the possessor of a winning personality. Realizing that there was great need for educated men in South Carolina, decided to follow the worthy example of his older brother. In the fall of '22 he entrusted his potentially great intellect to the "Wise Heads" of C. A. C., in the hope that they would mold it into a resourceful thinking anatomy. It can be readily seen now that the profs have been very successful. He has taken an active part in the literary societies, the Y. M. C. A., and religious work in general throughout his college career. He is noted for his humorous and witty sayings, his friendly disposition, and his cheerful smile, all of which have made for him a host of true friends.

name: Gaffney, Harris Earle
motto: Life is a short day; but it is a working day.
clubs: Operator College Broadcasting Station three years
Expert Machine Gunner, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: This versatile lad hails from the town that bears his name. Since joining the Tiger Clan, he has shown that he is a hard worker, a sincere friend, and a real Tiger. He joined the "electron seekers" and his interest in electricity can be shown by his efficient service in helping to establish a broadcasting station at Clemson. As literary editor of "The Chronicle," he deserves much credit. Largely through his efforts "The Chronicle" has been placed among the best of college publications. As one of the premier members of the cadet band and concert orchestra, he has helped promote better music at Clemson. We fear not for him, for we realize that a man of his ability, determination, and high ideals will make a success.

name: Garrison, Charley Reid
motto: Be true, for there are those who trust you; Be pure, for there are those who care; Be strong, for there is much to suffer; Be brave, for there is much to dare.
clubs: Member, Junior and Senior Dancing Clubs
President and Recording Secretary, Columbian Literary Society
description: Charley came to Tigertown in the fall of '23, and due to his superior intellectual ability, he will be ready to depart from the Tiger Lair with the Class of '26. As a student Charley studies hard and makes good grades; as a friend he is always willing to lend a helping hand and is loved by all who know him; as a musician he is of the very best and the Clemson band will deeply regret his leaving; as a gentleman he is second to none. He is strictly a ladies' man. His one great failing is girls; but his wonderful "sheikish line," especially when on Glee Club trips, has made his failing a great success. Many of the fair damsels from the surrounding colleges have imagined that they reign supreme in Charley's heart. Genial, good-natured, always with a smile for every one, he is just the type of man to succeed in whatever he undertakes. Charley, we wish you much success in life, the highest school of learning.

name: Garrison, Norman Asa
motto: I'd rather have the love of a true girl, Than all the riches of the world.
clubs: Sophomore, Junior and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: It is inconceivable just how many friends this loyal Tiger possesses. They can easily be accounted for, however, by his charming personality and real true friendship. Norman is the type of gentleman that we just like and respect. Promptness, neatness, perseverance, and natural ability which have, incidentally, warranted his military achievements, are his dominant characteristics. We can only predict that these qualities will also help him in winning the hand of one worthy of him. Norman's ability and determination will win for him a place in the Electrical world paralleled only by those of great fame. We can only hope that the leaders of this profession will recognize his sterling qualities as did his instructors at Clemson.

name: Gignilliat, George Warren
motto: "Some for the Glories of this World; and some Sigh for the Prophets Paradise to come; Ah, take the Cash, and let the Credit go, ..."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer International Relations Club
Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: Reserve and dignity characterize every step that Gee has made at Clemson. Versed in all the wiles of social life, abundantly supplied with liberal knowledge, and gifted with a mind that has never found a problem too profound to solve, he has filled countless positions of responsibility and capability. Never overbearing or presumptive, but always calm and impressive, Gee rightly suggests tremendous reserve power of personality and ability. His talents are wide-spread, but his greatest achievements here have been in scholastic life, and in a managerial capacity among student organizations. He has piloted this 1926 Taps to a grand finish, and he has successfully guided our best social functions for well-deserved success.

name: Gilmer, Frederic Swain
motto: "The universe is change; our life is what our thoughts make it."
clubs: Member Sophomore, Junior and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: The best study of mankind is man. Here is a real study of mankind in the person of Frederic Swain Gilmer. An ounce of wit and a single smile are worth a pound of sorrow, and in Fred may be seen a daily practice of this old proverb. Ever lighthearted, ever hopeful, having always an irresistible smile for everyone, he continually chases gloom. Possessed of inherent grace and ease of manner, Fred is at ease with whatever class of society he may be thrown, whether it be in the ball-room or in the cabin. Perhaps one of his best attainments is his power to divine human character; and his opinions, formed not hastily, are to be treated with utmost respect for their unerring accuracy and truth. Strong of initiative, of him may well be said: He dares to do, for he who dares not is lost. If natural ability, together with the better qualities which go to make a real man, be any measure of success, Fred shall indeed attain the unattainable.

name: Greene, George Hembry
motto: Trust everybody, but always cut the cards!
description: When Judge was a freshman and decided to take Electrical, some Sophomore gave him a big stick to clear the light of protons. Judge suspected a joke somewhere, and used a pair of rubber gloves instead--afterwards adding an e to his last name. George is a good student, and (if he doesn't rush for the Klondike) will make good in his chosen field of Electricity.

name: Hall, Frank Burris
motto: Friends keep the pathway of life lighted.
description: Yes, here he is, the fellow that all Iva has watched for the last four years with pride. Frank came to Clemson with two strong determinations: to get his diploma, and to put Iva on the map. He has fulfilled his first wish and nothing but time can keep him from carrying out his second. For three years Frank has been busy working problems in electricity about magnetism and resistances. For the last year he has been busy with a problem about magnetism and resistance--but of a different nature. It is whispered that this problem refers to a certain young lady at Winthrop whose magnetism he thinks will help him put away all resistance he meets in after life. Iva, we are returning to you a man, graduated in Mechanical Engineering, in return for a boy that you sent to us four years ago. His success we assure you.

name: Hane, John Keitt Jr.
motto: God and the world loves a fighter—not the kind that will bravely lie down and die, but the kind that fight like hell to live.
description: Physically, a man; morally, a gentleman; at heart, a true Tiger. After he had become acquainted with Clemson and was properly imbued with the Tiger spirit, he decided to join the ranks of those who uphold the Purple and Gold on the gridiron. Since he made this decision, Kit has inhaled the Hurricanes, torn the Blue Hosiery, plucked Gamecock feathers, and pulled Bulldog tails. In the field of this activity, he has always fought like a real Tiger. This lad is an ardent admirer of the fair ones and we predict that in the near future some sweet girl will also be a lucky one. Kit, a boy with a keen intellect, a striking personality, and a strong character, is the sure bet to add to his name the glory that a successful Clemson man is sure to gain.

name: Hane, Whitfield Watson
motto: Friendship above all ties does bind the heart; and in friendship is the noblest part.
description: Behold! A man, and every inch of him is just that. Since coming to Clemson, Whit has proved to us that because of his ability, he is just as much at ease in the classroom, on the dance floor, on the campus, or anywhere else he might be thrown, as he would be in his own home. Seldom do we find a man with such a blending of these characteristics. His personality is a wonderful combination of the factors mentioned before, and this alone has endeared Whit in the hearts of us all. In Whit we expect to see the development of a second Burbank. With years of experience and a Clemson diploma as his backing, nothing short of death itself can hinder his accomplishments in the horticultural field. Watch him; it will be well worth your attention.

name: Harrelson, Ottis Miles
motto: I shall not predict my failures in life by remaining idle, but will strive to make my success in life proportional to the talents which God has given me.
description: In the good old year of 1922, left his home in The Independent Republic of Horry and began his journey to Tigertown. During his stay in Clemson, he has been a consistent and enthusiastic supporter of all Clemson teams. Ottis makes friends readily, and once a friend, always a friend. He not only is popular at Clemson, but he is also greatly loved and admired over at Lander and at G. W. C. We feel sure that he will prove to be the Edison of Horry County, and we predict for him a successful future. The very fact that he was elected president of his county club without a dissenting vote proves the esteem and love with which he is regarded by his fellow students.

name: Hawkins, Charles Elry
motto: A woman's counsel brought us first to woe, And made her man his paradise forego.
clubs: Manager Clemson Activities, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Here, folks, is none other than H. Lloyd Hawkins, and never shall we see his like again. Harold joined us in the fall of '22 and with the exception of a few weeks vacation, taken at the instance of the Discipline Committee, has followed us straight through. Harold always thought for himself, acted accordingly, and reckoned not the consequences. A glance at the above list of offices he has held will show what his fellow students think of him. He knows the valence of the several elements, and when he makes his bow to the field of Chemistry, we predict some great advances in that science. Go to it, Harold. We wish for you all the success in the world.

name: Heller, Walter Furman
motto: "If sleep be music, play on."
description: To the college, as well as to his class, Walt, throughout his entire course, has always shown himself to be a loyal indispensable asset. He has consistently displayed real ability as a student. He is a friend to everyone, and to number his is to number all those with whom he has come in contact. In the classroom, Walt proves that he is one of the most brilliant members of his class. He is one of the few students who have never made an unsatisfactory grade, and this is an achievement of which he has reason to be proud. With his pleasing personality, versatility and determination, the Class of '26 can predict for him only success in the Electrical World.

name: Hendrix, Samuel Herbert
motto: "Not on the heights, but climbing."
description: Step up, ladies, and take a look at the original sheik of Lexington. No, he was not the one who fought the battle of Lexington because he has been too busy "shooting" his professors—and that's not all he shoots either. It is rumored that he has a wicked line, and he knows where to shoot that and also how. Snake attended camp at Plattsburg and it is rumored that he made several trips across the border into Canada. We like to wonder—? Snake is a prince of a fellow and we are going to miss him, but our loss will be a gain to someone else. We wish you the best of luck, "Snake," and we know you'll get it.

name: William Thornwell Henery
motto: I'd rather have the love of a true girl than all the riches of the world.
clubs: Pistol Expert R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Henery became tired of "watching Sedalia grow," so he came to Clemson in search of knowledge. Sedalia is not through growing, but judging from his flow of sesquipedalian words, the search is over. He is a loyal Tiger, a true friend, and a gentleman. He is a very efficient scholar, and is an ardent supporter of student activities. Early in his college career Henery elected Entomology for his life's work, and the elements that have prompted him to the place he holds in the hearts of his friends will surely push him up to the last rung in the ladder of success. He is the very reason why girls leave home. From the way he talks, we judge that his heart-smashing tactics are over, and that he is ready to settle down with the choice of his heart. Luck to you!

name: Jackson Bowling Hester
motto: Do it now; delay is dangerous.
clubs: Life Saving Corps, Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Jack came to Clemson with the sole idea of making a chemist of himself; and we may truthfully say that he has succeeded in building a foundation that is capable of withstanding the hard knocks of life. We feel sure that with the good disposition, the ability to make and to hold friends, and the determination to achieve the final goal, great success will follow whatever he may pursue. The best of luck to you, Jack. The Class of '26 will always cherish your memory.

name: Hinson, Harry Sylvester
motto: Success is sure if one will only trust and give his all.
clubs: President and Vice-President Lee County Club
description: Here is a gentleman of the highest order. Harry came here for one great purpose—to become an eminent Horticulturist. One has but to watch him in his work to realize that he will not fall short of his aim. If hard work, sincerity, and tenacity are requisites of success, Harry must succeed. Harry is a friend to all who know him, and all know him. He is a favorite among the fair sex. A friend to all, honest of convictions, sincere of purpose, of high ideals, a jolly good fellow with a winning personality; this is Harry. Men who know Harry best realize full well that a more genial friend cannot be found. And we expect him to be rewarded with the same degree of success in the world as he has attained in college. Here's to you, Harry. May you, in producing more and better fruit, be endowed with the richest blessing and happiness.

name: Hope, Robert Hervey
motto: He who only hopes is hopeless.
clubs: Rifle Marksman R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Those who know "Bob" wish for no better friend. To say he is a prince of a good fellow would be putting it mild. His attracting personality, his ability to think for himself, and his judgement stamp him as a man loved and respected by all. Bob came to Clemson from Rock Hill in the fall of '22 to add to his already large store of knowledge. During his four years at Clemson he has taken part in many and varied college activities. His classmates have affectionately dubbed him "Hopeless," which is probably not very erroneous, for we know that he is hopelessly in love with a certain fair one at Winthrop. If Bob makes as good a teacher as he does a hot air artist, there will be no limit to his success.

name: Batson, John Preston
motto: "Tis better to lose with a conscience clear than to win by trick unfair."
clubs: Calhoun Literary Society Junior Literary Critic, '23
President, Secretary and Treasurer Greenville City Club
Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: Johnny came over from the Textile Center in 1922 and started working. He left school a short while in his Senior year, but he returned again this session and is gradually resuming his former efficiency. He possesses great oratorical ability, and is recognized as an efficient executive. He delves fruitfully into social life and is a great participant in athletics, although he has not won the much desired block letter. Always smiling and good natured, he forms many friendships, yet he always leaves an impression of dignity and ability that is never too deeply overshadowed by his jollity. He proceeds through life like a pedestrian in a crowded thoroughfare. Turning here, stopping only to secure a stronger start, he always holds to a definite course which cannot help but lead to success and happiness. Some of his classmates may achieve greater financial success, some may hold more glory, but the joy and peace that go with a well-lived life will be Johnny's reward.

name: Boseman, Thomas Raymond
motto: "When sailing down the stream of life, paddle your own canoe; And never trouble trouble, till trouble troubles you."
description: This lad hails from the city of Darlington. He came to Tigertown in the fall of '22 with the sole intention of mastering his chief and foremost aim of life, mechanical engineering, and so far has proved successful in every attempt. Raymond is a true friend, a loyal Tiger, and a man who is always ready to help anyone who may be in need. He was never known to give up a task until satisfied that he had done his best. The fairer sex has never, as yet, worried Raymond very much; but there is one in Darlington who wishes him all success in life. His belief is that before and after conquering life's work that the rest will come easy. Luck is to you, Boseman. May your joys be as big as the ocean and your sorrows as light as the foam.

name: Jackson, Ralph Alexander
motto: Every complete work requires a complete man.
description: Ralph doesn't consider it necessary for a man to change his mind every day. A consistent friend and a valuable one is Jack. Jack has been one of the mainstays of Coach Goodale's team for three years. His comments on football and the "Ag" Profs are always interesting—he knows something of them all. If Jack chooses to follow up his chosen work of education, we predict success for him. A man who can practice what he preaches and never lose sight of the "goal post," can be nothing other than a successful man. That man is Jackson.

name: Jackson, Thomas Gilbert
motto: Defer not to be wise, Tomorrow's sun to thee may never rise.
clubs: Member Sophomore, Junior and Senior Dancing Clubs
Secretary and Treasurer Junior Dancing Club
description: The first thing that impresses you when you meet Gilbert is his bubbling, irrepressible good nature. Never worried, never cynical or pessimistic, he spreads good feeling wherever he goes. His whole person is a pleasing combination of well blended characteristics. He merges his wit with wisdom, and combines his executive ability with a winning personality. When he arrived here, he swung into the routine of scholastic duties with his characteristic vigor and climbed swiftly to the top. At ease in the best social circles, happy among the less formal, he typifies the best in democracy. He hides his serious mind behind his jollity, and smothers his scholastic excellence under an avalanche of varied duties and campus activities.

name: Jones, William Luther
motto: The motto of chivalry is also the motto of wisdom; serve all, but love only one.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Greenville County Club
description: In the fall of '22 there came to Clemson from the city of Greer this genuine specimen of manhood. He decided to cast his lot with "Crip" and he is now in "Crip's" opinion, a rival of the class of '06. He has many friends, due to the fact that he will stand by one through thick or thin. He is a little man with a big heart. His excellent character and good-natured ways will no doubt make a success for him, and we all wish him all the success and happiness in the world.

name: Jordan, Edward Harold
motto: My all is not much, but I give that winning or losing.
clubs: Recording Secretary, President Calhoun Literary Society
Secretary and Treasurer Darlington County Club
description: All hail the power when a genial, faithful lad ascended from the vicinity of Timmonsville to cast his lot with the Clemson Tigers. While Clemson has heartily welcomed her gain in capturing this man, his home town has grieved over a great loss. Embodying the natural elements of a true being; congeniality, sincerity, personality, spirituality, and friendly demeanor, have won him a vast number of friends—and he has the ability and tact which enables him to keep them. The qualities of a natural leader with which he is possessed have elevated his ambition to the honorary position of Chaplain of "King Cole's Army;" and also the Presidency of the Y.M.C.A. May your good work prosper forever. To you and yours, our sincere wishes are everlasting for your happiness and success in this great universe of trials and temptations. Now may your troubles all be small ones and your life fruitful.

name: Jordan, Robert Calhoun
motto: "Count that day lost when the low descending sun views from thy hand no worthy action done."
clubs: First Class Gunner, Howitzer Company, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: On one bright September morning four years ago, there was great sorrowing in the little town of Richburg. Bob was leaving for Tigertown. Upon entering Clemson, he immediately cast his lot with the seekers of fame in the agricultural world. Here we find he has made a good record. In his Junior year he became an ardent disciple of "Sooky." In his class work he is excelled by no one, and if hard work and the ability to accomplish things count for anything in life, we are confident that he will be a success in his particular field. During his four years among us, he has become a true Tiger, ever ready to help Clemson in any way he can. To know Bob is to know a true gentleman. His high ideals make him loved by all his acquaintances. Although Bob is business-like, he never allows it to interfere with his pleasures. No matter how busy he may be, he has time to write to Lander College. We predict in the near future a cute little bungalow for two.

name: Kearse, Francis Grier
motto: "Do unto others as you would have others do unto you."
description: As a member of the class of '26, Kearse has proved himself worthy. Though never deeply affected with the desire for military fame, Daddy Trent burdened him with a lieutenancy, but this undeserved glory was removed from his shoulders by Colonel Cole, who reduced him to a private. Kearse is quite handsome and is the possessor of many winning ways. We find that the girls also fall for him. Believing himself quite competent, and realizing the possibility in the electrical field, he hung out his shingle in the Electrical Laboratory. Besides learning the nature of the molecule, he has learned to chase them along with other interesting things. Though he may fail to continue his work in Electrical Engineering in after years, we all feel sure that his policy of doing the right thing by his fellow men will continue to stand him in good stead under all conditions and circumstances in life.

name: Belton, David King
motto: "They who give have all things; they who withhold have nothing."
description: "B. D." as he is better known, hails from Swansea. After completing his high school days he decided to come to tiger-town to broaden his mind in the field of Electricity. King, or "B. D.", as we know him, chose the course he liked best and has been a faithful worker, as shown by his "S's" and "F's" during the last two years in college. This lad is a real king to his friends, and this is well shown by the number of friends that he has among his fellow students. It can be said of King, and well, that he is a true friend of everyone who knows him. Here's to you, "B.D.", and we predict that your future will be very successful.

name: Hugh, Franklin Kizer
motto: "Women are but mere toys to amuse men's lighter hours; Ambition is the serious thing of life."
description: Hughie, as he is called by his many friends, was among those who answered the call for loyal Tigers in the fall of '22. He joined the Education division, and with the grim determination to master wisdom, he cast aside love, romance, and women, in the pursuit of truth and knowledge. Hughie, throughout his college career has been an honest and faithful worker, and is worthy to be called a true Clemson Tiger. The people of St. George should lift their hats to this lad because he has not only been successful in the scholastic world, but his ideals are high and he is a man above par. With his strong determination and winning personality, we can predict nothing but success for him in the future. Here's to you, Hughie; luck, success, and happiness. The class of '26 will cherish your memory.

name: Williston Wightman Klugh, Jr.
motto: Take up no more than you by worth may claim, Lest soon you prove a bankrupt in your fame.
description: "Woots," as he is known to us, is from the heart of Tigertown. Among the red heads, no more jolly fellow can be found than this sorrel-topped Irishman. After entering Clemson in '22, he became disgusted and decided that a tour of the East should be made before retiring and absorbing an education. He found after completing his tour that there was no place more suited to a true Tiger than in his own lair, and he returned to us and began to work with extra vigor and enthusiasm. "Woots" shows what a real man can do; he forgot that he lost three months of valuable time, caught up with his original class and graduated with honors. Clemson athletic teams will miss him sorely and the memory of the boy with the cheerful disposition will long be cherished by the Class of '26 as one of its most loyal members.

name: Jack McDonald Law
motto: The moving finger writes; and, having writ, Moves on; nor all your Piety nor Wit Shall lure it back to cancel half a line, Nor all your tears wash out a word of it.
clubs: Senior Critic, President Calhoun Literary Society
Member Sophomore, Junior and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: Possessed of a disposition that is admired wherever he is known, Jack, with his witticisms, has been the life of the party during his four years sojourn in Tigertown. His smile and his ability to look on the bright side of the seemingly dark spots in life has consoled those who often drift into a solemn, semi-pessimistic state of mind. However, Jack has not allowed his fun-loving disposition to hinder his progress in life, and since coming to Clemson he has passed through the stages of development with a success which is a matter of pride to all his many friends. Unless some unforeseen obstacle, such as a beautiful young lady, arises to block the advance of Jack, we fail to visualize a defeat for this smiling lad in the battle for success.

name: Leitzsey, Frederick Byron
motto: Salt your food with humour, pepper it with wit, and sprinkle it over with human kindness.
description: Fred is the lad who keeps Tigertown roaring. No doubt you have heard of him before, either as an athlete, a comedian, songster, or student. In fact there are few things that Fred can't do. Because of his unusual ability and clever personality, Fred holds a dominant place in the hearts of his classmates. Fred is a real fighting Tiger. He proved this conclusively this year when he remained out for football the entire season with a badly injured knee. His voice of husky sweetness, together with his ability to shoot a line has been to his advantage in entertaining the fair sex. Whether Fred will follow Chemistry or go on the stage, we do not know; however we do know that he is certain to succeed in whatever he chooses.

name: Lemmon, Junius Mayes
motto: One thing is forever good; That one thing is success.
clubs: Member Junior and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: Junius Mayes Lemmon, better known under the cognomen of "Physicue," is the one absolutely unique character in the Class of '26. Troubles may pile up, everything may go dead against him, the silver may disappear from the clouds, but "Physicue" just smiles through it all. One thing, however, must be admitted; he is not perfect. Better than that he is unfailingly human, and it is this trait which has hoisted him to the positions of responsibility which he has held among his classmates. Some people are so great that they forget their companions; others are so low that they forget everything but pleasure; "Physicue" strikes the happy medium. He is perpetually happy, and if happiness is success, this lad who has tasted deeply of all the activities which a course at Clemson affords, is well on his way to a ripe, well rewarded career.

name: Li, Ko-Chia
motto: Whang,
description: Ko-Chia crossed the Pacific in 1922, to invade the centers of learning in America after he had been prepared for higher learning in his homeland. For two years he attended the Lowell Textile School in Massachusetts, and then came to the North Carolina State College to continue his studies in Textile manufacturing. (meaning emperor in Chinese) is a familiar figure on the campus, being especially noted for his achievements in tennis and ping-pong. Everywhere he makes friends, for he is a jolly, fun-loving friend to everyone, and his ready laugh and numerous witticisms have made him as well liked as he is well known. One trait of his must be mentioned; he is tremendously girl-shy. His nick-name, "Whang," is appropriate for with his natural ability plus his ability to work, we predict that he will rise to great heights in his chosen field. Here's to you, Ko-Chia, we who have known you shall not like to see you part, but when we do part our best wishes for your success go with you.

name: Long, L. S.
motto: Listen to every man's advice, but back your own judgment.
description: In September 1921, when L. S. Long packed his grip and left Prosperity for Clemson, no truer Tiger ever entered here. He enlisted with the Animal Husbandry Division, and here he has been an efficient and able worker, putting that enthusiasm and ability in mastering his profession which assures him of a prosperous and successful future. Long was forced to withdraw from his class, but when the fall of '25 rolled around, he was back again. He is a congenial companion, a true friend, a loyal Tiger, an able student, and, above all, an honorable gentleman.

name: McAllister, Lawrence Clayton Jr.
motto: Never put off until tomorrow that which you can do today.
description: When he came to Tigertown in the fall of '22, he thought "Autonomous grandis" was a new kind of opera; but nevertheless he chose "Bugology" as his life work. He isn't what we call a sheik, but occasionally he steps out among the ladies, thus demonstrating what his sheiking qualities might be. His scholastic record shows that he has a chosen field and that he has chosen a course in which he is deeply interested. He is not only a good student, but also a strong supporter of all Tiger teams. Yes, he is a gentleman, a true friend and a true Tiger; and we do not hesitate to say that the same traits that have raised him in the hearts of his friends will surely place him on the top rung of the ladder of success. Here's hoping that you will put Pendleton on the map in the near future.

name: McClellan, Guy William
motto: Do unto others as you would have them do unto you.
description: Look, friends, here is a true Tiger. This lad did not win the distinction of being a true fighting Tiger on the athletic field, but he has shown his stuff by entering Clemson from the ninth grade of high school and keeping a clean sheet all the way through his college career. No doubt everybody has heard that determination is the master key to success; well, here is a living proof of the statement. He entered Clemson in the fall of 1922 with one thing in view and that was to get an education. By the end of his Sophomore year, he concluded that he was capable of carrying on two jobs at the same time. To our surprise, he chose as his hobby the difficult task of winning a girl's heart. From our point of view, we believe that success has come his way; and we are anxiously waiting and watching to see what he will undertake next. A friend in need is a friend indeed, and you have proved yourself worthy of one's friendship.

name: McCormac, Eugene Little
motto: Sound, sound the clarion, fill the fife; To all the sensual world proclaim, One crowded hour of glorious life Is worth an age without a name.
clubs: President, '26, Secretary and Treasurer, '25, Columbian Literary Society
description: In the fall of '22 "Mac" came to Clemson from the wilds of the surrounding territory from his home in Dillon, and here for the past four years has been engaged in the task of acquiring higher learning. Mac was endowed by nature with a sunny disposition, and this, in addition to his happy personality, has made a host of real friends among the fairer sex. Throughout his college career Mac has allied himself with the best influences on the campus, and has been an active worker in the Y. M. C. A., in his church, and in the various societies of which he has been a member. So it is an enviable position which Mac holds with his fellow-classmen. Life holds a great many fine things in store for you, Mac. We predict for you a great future, a future which has been foreshown by the life you have lived here at Clemson.

name: McCraw, Leslie Gladstone
motto: To live as gently as I can; To be, no matter where, a man.
clubs: Rifle Marksman R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: "Mac" left Gaffney in '22 to try his luck with the Clemson Tigers and he has been a success ever since then. He was handicapped when he joined us, but by perseverance and real work as only Mac knows how to accomplish, he has mastered every undertaking that he has attempted. Leslie's pleasant smile and friendly laugh win him admission everywhere. This lad has been under the "Lord's" care, but in spite of this we feel confident that the only reason Mac does not do something big is because it has all been done. He has one recreation, and it claims him every night. This is a little lady in Sandy Springs who hears from C. A. C. daily. We predict immeasurable success and happiness for you, Mac.

name: McGee, Ellas Thomas
motto: Life is just what you make it.
description: The little city of Starr gave up a worthy son when he decided to enter Clemson in the fall of 1922. Since that time he has gained many good friends and proved himself to be an earnest worker. He has also done much for his fellow students. Upon entering Clemson, he decided to take Agriculture as his life work. He was very successful as an "Ag," but soon found out that Civil Engineering was better suited for a man of his ability. Classwork comes first with him, but it is rumored that he always finds time to write to that fair maiden back home. He loves that "Fair One" like a baby loves milk. We wish you the best of luck in your future work. May success be yours in whatever you undertake to do.

name: McGill, Clarence Allen
motto: Do something worth living for, worth dying for; do something to show that you have a mind, a heart, a soul within you.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Anderson County Club
description: This, dear reader, is Clarence McGill, better known as "Mac," of whom the class of '26 is justly proud. He hails from the Electric City and does not regret that fact. He is known to be a friend by all who know him. He is a good enemy, a true sport, a true friend, and a hard fighter. With these qualities, ones opinion of him can be nothing less than superb. If friendship may be reckoned the masterpiece of nature, we certainly have such in Mac. Clarence says that he belongs to her, but Clemson claims him after he has gone into wider fields. Your natural ability, combined with the inspiration of that little girl, is sure to place you on your feet.

name: Bouknight, Luke Smith Jr.
motto: Let me live by the side of the road and be a friend to man.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer, Saluda County Club
Secretary and Treasurer, Twin City Club
Rifle Marksman, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: This young man came to Clemson from Batesburg in the fall of '22 to prepare for a great agriculturist; but he soon changed his course to Civil Engineering. From all present indications, he will make a great success as a Civil. He has made good in his classes by his ability to stick to a thing until it is finished. He is a man of good character, with but few bad habits and with many good ones. Due to his pleasing disposition and good character, he has made many friends during his sojourn at Clemson. He has also won one little heart at G. W. C. We all unite as one in the hope that he may have God-speed in his climb to the peak of success.

name: Bowles, Horace Julian
motto: To be, do, for the fight has just begun.
description: Julian first gained renown at Clemson by his athletic ability, but this was only an introductory paragraph to his volume of life here. A titan of strength, he has learned to control such force and convert it into useful, efficient work. He has the admirable quality to stick to a thing and carry it through, regardless of the circumstances or facts. His cheery mien is an inspiration to his comrades. Work never daunts him, and his willingness to perform his duty to assist his friends in any unselfish endeavor makes him popular with his smaller and less gifted friends. He is the recipient of much rightly placed confidence, and his square dealings with his fellow students have placed him in exacting positions of responsibility.

name: Jennings, Bryan McKerley
motto: Happiness is not the end of life; character is.
clubs: Clippings and Comments Editor, The Chronicle
description: The brains of that group of students who are so frequently caught winking at the butt-end of a transit, was one of the smallest, but most spirited rats of '22. His home town, Blackville, has withstood his absence for four long years—how, I don't know, and I am sure he himself would not tell, since he is more concerned with the fair sex than he is with himself. Mac's small body holds a magnificent heart and stands under a head which holds a brilliant mind, both of which working together, make him a southern gentleman. His record as a student, his perseverance as a worker, his ability as a leader, and his determination to succeed predict for him a future which we must say is well deserved, and which cannot be too successful.

name: Maner, James King
motto: An honest man, buttoned close up to the chin, no broadcloth without, but a warm heart within.
description: In the fall of '21, James King Maner fared forth from his home environment, not because he wanted a better place to live, but for the very good reason that he was ambitious. His ambitions are not few in number, but his present ambition is to become an agriculturist through the Animal Husbandry gate. He is small in stature, possessed of a wonderful personality, courteous nature, friendly disposition, and of versatile ability—ever a winner of anything he undertakes. It has been his good fortune to win a host of friends who learned to know him only to love him. When cruel fate extracted its toll from the Class of '25, J. K., believing that a man should be true to his word and should take a firm stand in upholding his convictions, left college. Ere the Class of '26 was well on its last lap, he was a member. As you go forth on life's journey from this institution of learning, we are certain that only success can crown your efforts.

name: Martin, Benjamin Vincent
motto: In God I trust and marry I must.
description: The above advertisement represents a true Tiger from the wilds of Jungletown. To know Ben is to like him, as is evident by his host of friends in barracks and on the campus. His magnetic personality, versatility, and determination to successfully accomplish what he starts has made for Ben a record of which he may be justly proud. Ben entered Clemson with one object in view, and that was to master Electricity, and for his past record he is admired by "Crip" as were Gandy and other brilliant members of the class of '06. Ben has that determination, that fearlessness, that concentrated energy, and that power of directness of life that spells efficiency, power, and success.

name: Mayfield, Judson Townes
motto: Everyone stamps his own value upon himself.
description: Judson, commonly known as "Jud," came to Clemson with the determination to become an Electrical Engineer. However, after three years of exposure to the electrical phenomena of his chosen course and the mysteriously worded lectures of "Slim," Jud decided that the electrical genial could best be investigated by some other than himself and subsequently changed his course to Industrial Education. We do not know just what this aggressive youngster's plans are for the future, but we believe that he intends to finish college, take his "dip" and himself to the lower part of the state, and offer them both to the discretion of "the only girl" upon the altar of love. Judson is a friend to all who are worthy of friendship, a good sport, and a conscientious worker. May success and happiness attend all his efforts towards whatever goal he should aspire.

name: Mobley, Francis Brumette
motto: The Goal's known, the Powers enlisted.
description: Special Student. He is a graduate of Furman University, having obtained his B.S. degree there in 1916. During his successful career there he made four block "F's" in baseball, each year making the All-State team. While there, he also made the varsity football squad. During the World War, Mobley served in the army for his country, spending much time overseas with the famous Thirtieth Division. He was wounded in action. After being discharged he worked for a time in a bank at Rock Hill. Mobley decided that he wanted to learn something about scientific agriculture, so he came to Clemson in September of 1923. Through concentrated efforts he has been able to finish his course here in three years. His scholastic record is one of which he has reason to be proud. Yes, he is a gentleman, a true friend, and a Tiger. He holds a place in the hearts of all those that know him. Never has he failed to accommodate a friend. His kindness and determination will attain success for him in whatever he undertakes.

name: Padgett, Andrew Burbidge
motto: Men are not judged by what they think.
description: Hark! "Red" heard the keen sound of a bugle. First call for his military career with an inspiring impulse. He then concentrated his attention upon the idea of becoming a Tiger and as a result cast his lot among the Palmetto Jungaleers. Red, being an ambitious lad, is bound to succeed, because his entrance to Clemson was his first step toward his goal, and each succeeding step has been an upward climb. Red is a good-natured fellow, well met, and has a smile for all with whom he comes in contact. He is a true Tiger, always ready to perform his duties and to lend a helping hand toward the up-building of his Alma Mater. Without a doubt Red realized the need of improved education in this state when he joined the Educational men—"a very select section." Loading his ship with knowledge, he will soon be ready to anchor in the harbor of life to import some of his gifts to the rising generation. Now, Professor, may your tools of knowledge be a blessing to you and to your pupils. Success for you is our fondest wish.

name: Palmer, Ernest Day
motto: He who fears ridicule is at the mercy of every fool.
description: Yes, Frog is a product of Central and she has reason to be proud of him. Frog has been prominent in his class because of his continual optimism and persistency in anything which he undertakes. Everybody loves a fighter. Frog doesn't know what quit means; and he has been a valuable man in football and basketball. He is a fighting tackle in football and a fitting man to the captain of the '26 Basketball team. Frog is very versatile and is competition for almost any team-mate. If he shows the same determination and desire to succeed after he leaves Clemson, we believe that great success awaits him only a short distance down the pathway of life.

name: Pauling, John Randolph Jr.
motto: He will never have true friends who is afraid of making enemies.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer, President, Dairy Club
description: That's a man from St. Matthews and don't forget it. Another fact to bear in mind is that St. Matthews is not swampy. Yes, Nig is a loyal South Carolinian and a dairyman combined. This lad has survived four years at Clemson although both students and profs have long looked for him to expire from having to climb the hills of the Piedmont. Show me a bull session and there I'll show you Nig, expert among the best. But Nig is not all bull; he believes in the future of dairying in South Carolina and when little Randolph enters Clemson, Nig will have become a power in the dairy business. Under all his bull and bluff, he is a pal, a friend, and a classmate never to be forgotten. According to Nig, there are three things to beware of: a woman, a rattlesnake, and a woman. Nevertheless, we feel sure that a certain little maid at Rock Hill will be able to change these revolutionary views.

name: Porter, Lester Allen
motto: "All may have, if they dare choose, a glorious life or grave."
clubs: Member Junior and Senior Dancing Clubs
Secretary and Treasurer Clean Sleeve Club
description: Modesty is the cloak of wisdom. Lester is the living personification of this. He is a very modest and unpretentious person, always having the highest respect for the feelings of those whom he comes in contact with. A lover of good times, his pleasing and unassuming personality has brought him many friends who admire him for what he is, without any false glamor to dazzle the superficial eye. Light-hearted, full of life, he is never worried about the future. He is an optimist and when things look their darkest to him he always has a smile to drive away the accompanying gloom. If to do were as easy as to know what were good to do, Lester would be premier of the land. If ever in search of a true friend and a real sport, it is useless to search for a superior to Lester. The dormant possibilities of his brain will one day astound the world.

name: Price, Launie Clarence
motto: "One who is contented with what he has done will never become famous by what he will do."
description: Not being a weight artist or a strong-arm man, "Red" did not "cast his lot" when he arrived at Clemson, but instead settled down to a doubtful pursuit of an electrical engineering course. In classes he has prospered; but he has reaped his greatest harvests in other, more remunerative fields. He was a stalwart figure on the gridiron in '25, and won fame for himself by driving through the fiercest defense. And similarly it will be in life—irresistibly onward regardless of opposition or handicaps. Then, too, Red is always cheerful—a redeeming feature in some people's lives, but an asset to this youth. Never selfish, but always ready to sacrifice for a companion, he has won many friends. Life cannot help rewarding him in due measure.

name: Pruitt, Joe Major
motto: I will speak today what I think even if it contradicts everything I said yesterday.
description: This lad is none other than Major Pruitt of whom the class of '26 is very proud and rightly so. Major is liked by everyone that knows him and, needless to say, he is widely known at Clemson. Before coming to Clemson, he attended B.M.I. for three years. He learned of the opportunities afforded at Clemson and decided to cast his lot with the Clemson Tigers. The numerous electric lights on the streets of the Electric City caused him to desire to know and have some knowledge pertaining to the whyfores. After hearing the musical sounds made by the machines in the Textile Building as he passed it on the way down town, he changed his mind and is now the proud owner of a diploma in Textile Industrial Education. We predict for this aggressive young man nothing less than the presidency of a big textile concern. Here's to you, Major and may success be yours.

name: Roche, Austin Oliver
motto: Do something worth living for; worth dying for.
description: "Monk," coming to us as a Tiger Cub in '22 from Abbeville, decided to do something shocking; consequently, he chose Electrical Engineering as his life work—quite shocking he assures us. Not being adapted to a military life, he quite readily added his favorite "Songster" to his schedule. We are certain that the "sweet young thing" of Abbeville is proof against male attractiveness, otherwise Monk could not have been with us. On good authority, however, she awaits his return with outstretched arms. His cheerfulness, his ability to make friends, his willingness to work, and his determination together make Monk. We cannot wish for him better than that his future holds for him the success which his characteristics predict.

name: Rogers, Hoyt
motto: Tarry not my lad; Love is a ticklish thing and you haven't even smiled.
description: In the early fall of 1922 "Rang," or Will, as he is known to his fellow Tigers, heard the Tiger's roar and taking up his musket, he started out to capture bigger game. He happened upon the Tiger's lair in the foot hills, and there instead of making a capture, he was captured himself. For four years "Rang" has searched in every corner for material that will better equip him for putting the "Flatwoods" around Mullins on the Agricultural Map. "Rang" is a great dreamer and an optimist. One of his greatest dreams is of a little nest somewhere in the east. He says that this would never be complete without a "Fair Bonnie Lass" to share in the realization of his dreams. Here's luck to you, "Rang;" may your best dreams come true; and may you not always be a dreamer, but a schemer and a thinker as well.

name: Roy, Wallace Robert
motto: If at first you don't succeed; try again.
clubs: Member, Relay Team holding American Record
description: This great athlete hails from the "Capital City." Since coming to Clemson, he has made a great success as an athlete, and few can surpass his record. On the football field, he has attained fame as an end and as a tackle. On the basketball court he has shown the goods. His outstanding records are on the track where he won the 440 yard dash at the Southern Conference Meet in 1924. He now holds the state record and the Clemson record for this event. This past year he was captain of the track team and has made a capable leader. Not only is "Wally" a good athlete, but he is a good student. In fact there are few things that this big boy can't do—and do well. He is admired and respected by all his classmates as an athlete, a student, and a gentleman. May success be yours, Wallace!

name: Salley, Edward McQueen Jr.
motto: It does not pay to worry; the world looks better from behind a smile.
clubs: Member Sophomore, Junior and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: Here's to the boy that you have heard so much about, and when you gaze at the picture above, you will find the real reason for his reputation. He is indeed a fine fellow, and the girls who have had the good fortune to meet him will agree with us in this statement. Speaking of girls—that's his weakness, and it seems that the fair sex also have a weakness when they encounter this fair lad whose favorite pastime is stringing a dozen at a time. We have often wondered who will be the lucky lady who will share with him the success and happiness that the future holds for him. He is made from a mixture of charm, originality, and neatness, plus sincerity. Such characteristics as these make up an all-round good fellow and one who is bound to succeed at the task he undertakes in life.

name: Sanders, Joe Hardin
motto: Be what you are.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Square and Compass Club
description: He hails from the center of the Catawba, the power center of the South. Better late than never must have rested hard upon his mind, for he waited a year after finishing Chester High School before starting his last lap toward knowledge. His quiet, unassuming manner, has won for him a warm place in the hearts of all who know him. He is not a brilliant student, but is steady and consistent, and has stood for the highest principles and done what he thought best regardless of the consequences. He leaves us as one of the best prepared men of his class in the Textile Industry, and we predict for him a bright future in his chosen field.

name: Sanders, James Robert
motto: Think before you make a promise; then stick to it.
clubs: Machine Gun Marksman R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Everyone take notice. This is Robert Sanders, better known to us as Bob, who caused a great commotion when he left Chester to enter Clemson. Bob is a big-hearted lad and has a great number of friends among the corps. Look into those eyes and you'll notice a far-away look in them. Possibly the cause of this is a little girl somewhere waiting for him. Bob came here with the intention of taking Electrical Engineering, but after chasing volts and electrons around for three years, he decided to change to Mechanical and find out all about B. T. U.'s and Entropy. We predict for this energetic Tiger a huge success and a career that is very seldom attained. Best of luck to you, Bob.

name: Sanders, Van Cortez
motto: Though wind and tides one compass guides—To that, and your own selves, be true.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Newberry County Club, '25
Rifle Marksman, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: "V. C." is a regular fellow, one who cannot be overlooked. He has a very pleasing personality and has won many friends at Clemson, as he does everywhere he goes. His success with the fairer sex is phenomenal. Although we have never found him conceited, we know that he does not mind losing a little beauty sleep. He has proved the truth of this many times by burning the midnight oil in his diligent pursuit of the elusive electron. Here's to you, V. C. May you always have success in the gratification of your ambitions.

name: Shands, Wayland Arthur
motto: There is a past which is gone forever, but there is a future which is still our own.
clubs: Member of Glee Club four years
Secretary and Treasurer Florence County Club
description: Wayland decided to launch his ship on the great ocean of life; realizing that he needed preparation, he set sail toward Clemson. He early found a liking for bugs, and hearing the call of nature, specialized in entomology. In the class-room, on the campus, and in the various clubs, he is a true friend, a faithful student and a faithful worker. Words fail us when we think of this man's career; we cannot say what he intends to do with his wonderful talent. His frequent visits to the various institutions of learning for women of this state—apparently on the Glee Club's business—bodes much evil for bachelorhood, and we predict the second period of life of man (according to Dr. Daniel). Remember, Wayland, we hold you in our hearts as a true friend, and wish for you the best of everything.

name: Sherman, Franklin III
motto: Love man, trust few, but always paddle your own canoe.
description: A hale fellow well met is "Shorty." His ability to mix with the fellows has won for him many friends who will always remember him. Here at Clemson he had made the marks of a learned scholar, and his word is respected by both the professors and the students. So luck to you Franklin! May your luck be evenly divided between Belton and Gastonia.

name: Boyd, James Arthur
motto: Make yourself an honest man and then you may be sure that there is one less rascal in the world.
description: Yes, folks, here is "King Arthur!" The lad with the sweet line and golden voice that we love so well. Immediately upon his arrival here four years ago he became endowed with the Clemson spirit, and since that time he has been a loyal and devoted Tiger. Arthur decided to take the "Bobbin-dodging" course and expose himself to helping handle the textile world. Not only is Arthur a good student, but a good singer as well. He has been one of the mainstays on the Glee Club. The little birds cease their singing and the little owlets cease their hooting when Arthur begins singing.
