Arthur is the possessor of a bright personality, having all the capabilities that go to make a real gentleman and a true friend. Here's to you, Arthur. May the best be yours and may she help you to build a castle of happiness in a sunny nook.

name: Bradley, Teofilo Leon
motto: Never act in the heat of emotion; let reason answer first.
description: "Prep," as he is known, hails from Columbia, "The City Unlimited." As a student Prep is not flashy, or exceedingly notorious, but he is a steady consistent worker. He is a fellow of whom we are all proud and one who always comes out on top. Prep has made for himself a warm place in the hearts of both the students and professors by his sterling character and genial disposition. His joking ways, his sarcastic manner, and seeming indifference, is well understood by all who know him and to know Prep is to know Clemson. It can be truly said that Prep is a sport, an athlete, and a gentleman of the highest order. We are looking to you, Prep, we know that you have the goods.

name: Simkins, Leroy Hampton
motto: "Nothing is so congenial as a clear conscience."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer, Aiken County Club
description: Not a scholastic prodigy, not an athletic marvel, this boy possesses a quality that is beyond the grasp of either of the former two. He has an unseen radiation of personal magnetism that thrust him forward to leadership and makes him one of Clemson's most trusted students. He has won a place for himself in the respect and in the hearts of his comrades by his unfailing convictions of thought and action. Being musical, his life follows the path of a rhythmic, well-balanced, and appealing melody. An unfailing sense of duty and the ability to enjoy wise pleasure gather him numerous friends. The glamor and artificiality of the bright lights has never blinded his sense of judgment. A companionable, hearty, and wholesome boy whose crowning attribute is unselfishness.

name: Smith, Barnard Milton
motto: "Believe every man to be your friend unless he proves to be otherwise."
description: This young lad came to our midst from the little town of Starr in the fall of 1922. Starr's loss was our gain, and ever since he has been a popular member of the class of '26. Since the day of his arrival at Clemson, he has been trying with all his might and power to broaden his knowledge as a Civil Engineer in order that the highways of the state may be improved. The frankness and sincerity that mark men of character are both of this lad. "B. M." with his unassuming ways, and thoughtfulness of everyone, has won many friends during his stay in Clemson. We wish you the best of luck that the world can offer a consistent fighter. We can vouch for your success.

name: Smith, Ernest Tyler
motto: To thine own self be true, and it must follow as the night the day, thou canst not then be false to any man.
clubs: Chief Bugler Drum and Bugle Corps
description: The big-hearted lad cast a shadow of gloom over the whole of Anderson County, including Iva, when he announced his determination to come to Clemson to become a Mechanical Engineer and to join Uncle Sam's Drum and Bugle Corps. Ever since his arrival here he has been a popular member of the class of '26. Judging from the letters he gets, he is also popular in other places. However, he does not let his social affairs interfere with his studies. His interest in student activities and his ability in class work have won for him the esteem and friendship of all who know him. Here's to you, luck, success, and happiness.

name: Smith, George Arthur
motto: Fail not in the harder trial; Faint not in the greater struggle.
description: He is a by-product of nature and the Anderson High School. He is truly an unsophisticated complex, his countenance always being blessed by a hydra-like grin, reminding one of "Happy" Higgins, the Canadian officer whose coat-tail was completely annihilated by machine gun fire at the Battle of Ypres. He derives his cognomen from the fact that he is the sole possessor of a catapult-like arm especially constructed for heaving hods and chunking baseballs. He began his career in the spring of 1923 by out-chunking Carolina to the tune of 6 to 0, no-hit no-run game. Since that time he has pitched steadily for the Tiger nine. He is a bobbin dodger of the highest degree. Some predict that he will become a great inventor, as it is rumored that he is working on an automatic tooth pick for textile workers. He is seriously handicapped by having been a "first looie" his Senior year. However, most likely he will fade away into oblivion within the next few years.

name: Smith, Junius Reid
motto: Do unto others as you would have them do unto you.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Greenville City Club
Rifle Marksman, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: In September 1922 Junius joined the happy family of Tigers at Clemson for the purpose of developing his mind along the lines of electricity. He managed to survive his Freshman year without any permanent injury, either mentally or physically. In fact, he learned several things that he was obliged to pass on to the next freshman class. In his Junior year he was a model of studiousness and perseverance. Then in the Senior Class he was one of those dignified ones who were just able to be human. Junius has a kind disposition, is generous, and has a manner about him that a certain young lady in another state seems to appreciate. If we were prophets we could say that fame would be his in life, but since we are not, we shall let him prove it to you.

name: Smith, Robert Eugene
motto: Determination makes dreams come true.
description: R. E. is one of the most versatile characters in our class. He is the possessor of a great number of the qualities that go to make a real gentleman. At all times he has been an energetic worker, a capable student, and a true son of Clemson. His cheerful attitude and sunny disposition have won for him a great host of friends at Clemson; friends who will always be true, because they know he will always be true to them. Smith's principal talent lies in the fields of art. As one of the most successful students in the Architectural department, he has been of great service to the "Taps" and to "The Chronicle." He is one man of whom we can truthfully say, that if he is ever given a task to do, he fulfills it, regardless of cost or sacrifice. Here's to you, old boy. We believe that there is a great future in store for you, and we expect some day to see you step into the shoes of Coles Phillips.

name: Smyth, James Adger Jr.
motto: Not afraid of work, but not in sympathy with it.
clubs: Machine Gun Marksman, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: In spite of the fact that he hails from North Carolina, Jimmy is a good boy. This, some say, is accounted for by the fact that he was born, and received his early training in the Palmetto state. Immediately after his arrival in Tigertown in the fall of '22, this smiling lad set to work as only he is capable of doing. Taking everything as it comes, rejoicing in good fortunes and never complaining of anything, all of which have been characteristic of Jimmie. Though seldom present for roll call at classes, Jimmie is always present and accounts for himself when the occasion arises. Though at local social occasions Jimmie has never shown himself to be much of a "ladies man," we suspect him of being a "bear" when away from home. In the coming years we shall watch with interest Jimmie's development into a good and useful citizen, husband and perhaps father.

name: Steer, Richard Dudley
motto: Whatever we want of any worth we've got to work to gain.
description: After wandering around a good portion of the Western Hemisphere, Dudley decided to cast his lot with us here at Clemson. And a very wise move he made, too, as a good boy and student like that which Dudley has proved himself to be, deserves to be trained in a real school. Dudley, after having grown up in cattle country and having been actively engaged in the cattle business, naturally took dairying as a duck takes to water. He is a real cow-man, a student, and a gentleman. While here at Clemson Dudley has made many friends by his qualities which also make him a true Tiger and a good man. He is a man as well as a scholar and he has those many other qualities that cause us to predict for him real success in life. Take him as he is—a real man.

name: Newton, William Stevenson
motto: Studious of good, he disregarded fame, And useful knowledge was his eldest aim.
description: Newt comes from the famous Electric City. In the fall of '22 when the "Blue Joy Special" left the Electric City, this young fellow was one of its passengers, going to that city known as Clemson's. Having felt the need of a mechanical engineer, Newt cast his lot with the Mechanicals, his intention being that of making success. Although small in stature, he is a big-hearted fellow who never troubles trouble, nor lets trouble trouble him. Newt is kind-hearted and falls in love easily when he meets young ladies. He has not been an athlete, but has won his fame in other ways, namely, that of being a successful member of the Glee Club, and a "wind-jammer." Here's to you, Newt. May success be yours as you journey along through life, and may you have no problems in your way, which are harder for you to solve than those given you in Mechanics and Steam.

name: Stewman, Walter Sidney
motto: Success in life is determined by what you do, not by what you say.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer, Vice-President Lancaster County Club
First Class Gunner, Howitzer Company, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Four years ago the population of Lancaster filed out to bid a fond farewell to a tall, lanky lad. This lad was none other than "Sid," as we call him, who was leaving for the Tiger Lair at Clemson. He came here with the sole desire to fit himself for the electrical world, and in response to his desire, immediately became one of the disciples of "Crip" and "Slim." He has created for himself a plane of prestige along these lines as well as along others. However, as time has passed, Sid's aspirations have shifted and now he plans to enter Uncle Sam's Army when he is finished. He has always been efficient in his military duties at school. To know Sid is indeed a pleasure; he believes in a square deal for every man. Although he has the army for his goal, he never allows this to detract his attention from other vital considerations. From all indications he has found something at Winthrop that inspires him to always rush for the evening mail. Here's luck to you, Sid.

name: Strickland, Paul
motto: The virtue lies in the struggle, not in the prize.
clubs: First Lieutenant, President Belton City Club
Rifle Marksman, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: A singular personality smiles in recognition of the chummy nickname. Not talented along any one line, yet he is slighted along none. A well-rounded, broad-minded character that keeps his cosmopolitan ideas well within the bounds of reason. Not overambitious, yet inspired to success by the lash of duty, self-respect and love of family, he is bound to succeed. Paul is cheerful, witty, and has a smile for all whom he greets. He is slow to anger, having a fiery temper well controlled. All these elements are interwoven in him, making him the attractive fellow that he is and winning for him a host of friends that will stick to him to the last ditch, confident that he will reciprocate.

name: Sudlow, William Harry
motto: What I must do is what concerns me—not what people think.
description: It was in the fall of '22 that Harry, better known to his many friends as "Sis," bade the fair ladies of Aiken good-bye and boarded the train for Tigertown. It is well that "Sis" chose to be one of "Crip's" disciples, for the Electrical World is badly in need of a man to fill the place of Steinmetz. We predict that the Electrical World will make way for Sis. Now Sis is just an all-round good fellow, a true friend, and a loyal Tiger. His friendly disposition, pleasing personality, and willing hand have won for him a host of friends at Clemson. With such characteristics as these nothing but success could be his. We believe in you, Sis, for a man of your ability and good traits is certain of success. May Clemson be proud of you in days to come.

name: Taylor, Walter Herman
motto: "Nulli sapere casu obtigit. No man was ever wise by chance."
clubs: Member Sophomore, Junior and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: Herman has never sought the fickle praise of the mass. He lives the old proverb that quiet waters flow deep. He has sought quiet, true friendships and his search has been bountifully prosperous. He revels in good literature, and his wide reading has given him an exceedingly broad view of life. Keen of perception, witty, impartial in opinions, and delightfully frank, he is possessed of a quiet culture that captivates friends but never offends the uninformed observer. Spot friendships are often blasted on better acquaintance, but the reverse is true of Herman. The transient nature of his humor, the biting delicacy of his criticisms may not be fully appreciated at first contact. To enjoy him most one must continually plumb the depths of his pleasing personality.

name: Thackston, Albert Jerome Jr.
motto: "Never let your looks interfere with your education."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Orangeburg County Club
description: Thax claims Orangeburg as his metropolis, and he is proud of his city and she should be proud of him. He is one of the most popular members of his class: "Such popularity must be deserved." The boys who know "Thax," and there are many, recognize in him the qualities of the highest type of mankind. He is a good sport, ready to give or take, never grouchy, and always smiling. Thax has given his attention to the whirl and hum of the dynamo, and from all indications, he will some day shock the world. With the determination and ability that is his, Thax will easily succeed in his chosen profession. The Class of '26 wishes you, Thax, a true Tiger, every success.

name: Thomas, Henry Lowry
motto: "What I must do is all that concerns me, not what people think."
description: "Tommie" comes to us from the city whose very name holds a thrill for him, and whose social life stands idle with open arms to receive again its inspiring leader who cast his lot with us in '22. Henry won a scholarship in Agriculture, but since he did not wish to become a tiller of the soil, he soon decided to specialize in a line which he calls, and somewhat pompously, Agricultural Chemistry, but which we simply call "a line." Call it whatever you will, he does not mind, for he has very definite ideas and ideals for his future in life. What more could we wish for you, Tommie, than that your future be filled with the success of your ideas and ideals which is so well deserved by your manifested manliness.

name: Tinsley, Harold Klugh
motto: "An honest heart and true love is all that life has to offer."
clubs: Corresponding and Recording Secretaries Agricultural Society
Corresponding Secretary and Vice-President Columbian Literary Society
Secretary and Treasurer Greenwood County Club
description: Harold, a boy of true worth and sterling character, whose words are bonds, whose oaths are oracles, whose love is sincere, and whose thoughts are immaculate, came to Clemson in the fall of '22 seeking a higher form of education. You may know him by his neatness, his sincerity, his great amount of energy, his steadfastness, and his gentlemanly conduct. Harold looks upon life seriously enough to make the proper choice, and lightly enough to be happy. He is an ardent admirer of the fair sex, and while they all seem to fall for him, there seems to be one who reigns supreme over his heart. With his superior mental ability, his stable character, his winning personality, and his high ambitions, Harold is sure to be a victor in life's battles. We predict for him a bright and prosperous future.

name: Tison, Perry Hamilton
motto: Choose that which is best and custom will make it the most agreeable.
clubs: Member Junior and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: He came to Clemson with the intention of taking "Bull," but realizing that he had failed to choose the best course, he changed to Horticulture. He is an excellent scholar, and we predict for him a great future as one of our leading horticulturists. He is a sincere friend and with his charming personality and winning ways he has won for himself a wide scope of friends. Tison is the same friend today, tomorrow, and whenever you see him. He always wins a host of friends wherever he goes. He never says much about his experience with the fairer sex, but we predict that there is one waiting his return, when they will take the final step. We are looking for great things from you, P. H. Life holds for you a great success and may the doors be opened to you.

name: Todd, Joseph Augustus
motto: To seek; to strive; and not to yield.
description: This young lad is Gus, another of those shining satellites of Starr. He came to Clemson in the Fall of '22, and after much thinking, finally cast his lot with the Mechanical Engineers. It is believed by all that this man will make a success because of the intense interest he shows in his work. Gus has made many friends during his stay at Clemson. He is a true friend, a loyal Tiger, and is always willing to help anyone who may be in need. It matters not how hard the problem may be, he always has stuck to it until it was finished. Luck to you, Gus. May your future be filled with joy and happiness.

name: Tozier, Leland Richard
motto: Blessed is he who expects nothing, for he shall never be disappointed.
clubs: Member, Beaux Arts Institute of Design
description: Lee, a boy from the Gamecock City, came to us four years ago to gain knowledge in architecture. It seems as if he has gained it, for he has already snatched a prize from nearly six hundred architects, by means of a splendid drawing. Keep it up, old man, you're doing splendidly. He is planning a trip to France to study next year—whether his wife is going or not, we can't say—she had better. Oh yes, he is married—tough luck girls! History repeats itself, therefore Michael Angelo and Betram Goodhue will have successors.

name: Trent, Roy Larkin
motto: Worse than a quitter is a man who is afraid to begin, for defeat is the lack of courage to try.
description: In the fall of '22, "Pop" migrated from the metropolis of Clifton and cast his lot at Clemson. He registered with the "Ags" and we now find him an outstanding member of the "Educations" and a devoted disciple of Tsukiyama. During his sojourn of four years, "Pop" has won a warm place in the hearts of his many friends. A loyal Tiger he is and when once known he is always liked because of his pleasing personality and sterling character. He is a hard worker and has accomplished great things in both the scholastic and social world, judging from the amount of his correspondence and his quaint expressions about love affairs. We believe that the near future must hold for him one joyous moment, when he shall confer the M. R. S. degree upon some fair damsel. With those traits of promptness, kind heartedness, and sympathy, we predict for him a happy and prosperous future. Here's to you, "Dad," the best of luck always.

name: Brown, Hugh Alexander
motto: Better do than dream; better be than seem.
description: Yes, this is Hugh, the man with such an open countenance. He is a man of high ideals and possesses a strong character. Hugh also has a wonderful attraction for the members of the fairer sex, among whom he has made many friends. He has a smile for everyone, and never fails to help the fellow who calls upon him for aid. Hugh came to Clemson to make good, and he has certainly done that. He was among the six highest rating students in the class during his Freshman year; and he has lived to that good record since that time. When we began our Junior year, Hugh cast his lot with the followers of "Sookie." We believe that he will make a wonderful professor and a leader of men. When he leaves us we shall certainly miss him. Hugh, we are looking to you to keep up the good work that you have started, and hope that you will have a long and successful life.

name: Bryan, William Wright
motto: The stalwart only stalk the hill... The game-fish only swim upstream.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Sophomore Dancing Club
description: Dignity is an asset to those capable of handling it in a manner that is fitting to all occasions; and even though "Baldhead" is six feet five inches in height, he has never lowered himself in passing under the gate-ways of life. Perhaps the outstanding feature in this lad's accomplishments at Clemson has been his work in the publication of our weekly paper, "The Tiger." "Baldhead" is an excellent journalist, and by his diligence and faithfulness, he has made his paper one of the best college publications in the South. As perhaps the future editor of some well-known daily, we can only wish him the success and happiness he deserves.

name: Trimmier, Lloyd Gignilliat
motto: God and the world loves a fighter—not the kind that will bravely lie down and die, but the kind that will fight like hell to live.
clubs: R.O.T.C., Camp Plattsburg Barracks, N. Y.
Pistol Marksman, Rifle and Automatic Rifle Sharpshooter
Second Corps Area R.O.T.C. Rifle Team, Camp Perry, Ohio, '25
description: The lad who hails from a well known city of South Carolina, arrived at Clemson with the determination to become a leader in the Textile Industry. Early in his career he departed from this idea and became a disciple of Mat Pearson. Trim has really accomplished wonders along this line, in that he is one of the best rifle marksmen in school. He had the honor of representing Clemson at Camp Perry, Ohio. This is something to be proud of. Trim had more than one thing in his cap when he went north in the summer of 1925. He claimed that he was going to Plattsburg, but the truth of the matter is that he went for the purpose of seeing a certain young lady in Brooklyn. Now, Trim, as each of us depart to our many walks of life, you carry memories of each of your fellow class-mates, and the assurance that the stage will be set for you to enter a state of matrimonial bliss.

name: Turner, George Edward Jr.
motto: Think before you make a promise, then keep it.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Electric City Club, '25
description: Came to Clemson in the fall of '21 from the Electric City, and has since been busy in the acquisition of higher learning. After two years, however, he was forced to stop out and work a year before completing his college career. This did not deter him from his purpose, and the next year he came back to finish the work that he had started. This shows what kind of a man he has proved himself to be, for it takes a lot of "stick-ability" to do what he has done. "Ed." is the kind of man that everyone is proud to know—honest, reliable, likable, and in all things a real gentleman. These traits of character have made him well liked by all who know him, and we are proud of having known G. E.—a real man.

name: Walker, John Emmett
motto: Fight with every ounce of strength until the final whistle; but then, if defeat comes your way—meet it with a smile.
description: This steel man left Blackville in the fall of 1922 to journey to Tigertown. After arriving he donned a football uniform and started out to make his block C. He showed up fine in 1923 and was a member of the South Carolina State Championship Team. He has been a very valuable member of the team, always showing the fighting spirit. He chose the Civil Engineering course as his college work, and it is very likely that he will succeed in life, as he has here. Winning the hearts of the ladies has been his side hobby, and from all appearances he has succeeded well. He never meets a stranger nor feels lost at any time. He adapts himself to his surroundings and enjoys himself. He is a jolly, friendly, and easy sort of fellow to get along with. The class of 26 sees no reason why he should not have much success in the future. Best wishes and good luck to you, Otey.

name: Watson, John Rice
motto: Mine honor is my life; both grown into one, Take honor from me, and my life is done.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Lexington County Club
Secretary and Treasurer Twin City Club
description: Batesburg-Leesville High School suffered a great loss in 1922 and Clemson made a great gain. John came to us with the determination to delve into the many mysteries of steam and Mechanical Engineering. He has worked persistently toward the attainment of the high ideal he has and has received the reward which we all covet. Work has not entirely taken up the time of John, for he has kept up a heavy correspondence with certain favored ones of the fairer sex. Which of them is the most successful we are not certain, but one will surely win soon. John, we shall miss you greatly next year, but we can bear our loss by remembering that the world will be greatly bettered by you. Here's to you and hoping that you will be as successful in the future as you have in the past.

name: Wells, Samuel Fogle
motto: Be what you really are.
clubs: Recording Secretary, Corresponding Secretary, Junior Literary Critic, President, Winner Declaimer's Medal, Calhoun Literary Society
Recording Secretary and Chaplain of Agricultural Society
Sharpshooter Machine Gun, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: To be a friend of "Queen's" is indeed to be a fortunate person, for no truer friend could anyone have. His magnetic personality and his determination to stick to the elevating things of life, have made for him a record of which he may be justly proud. He is the kind of fellow who says little but thinks much. Queen's interest in and loyal support of all Tiger Athletic teams has been outstanding in his career. His loyalty to them has been surpassed only by the loyalty which he has shown to his friends. He is also completely effused with an overwhelming interest in religious activities. Your fellow-students wish for you future success and happiness and a long and pleasant life. Your pure as gold qualities of promptness, perseverance, and frankness never fail to win their way in the game of life.

name: Westbury, Joseph Elmer
motto: Oh! would some Power the Giftie give us To see ourselves as others see us.
clubs: First Class Machine Gunner, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Unwavering adherence to high principles and altruistic tendency have won for "Whistleberry" a host of friends during his stay of four years in the army under the guidance of two strictly military leaders. Perhaps these two leaders gave him his military aspiration; perhaps he acquired them from some other source; but regardless of the source he is inclined to be soldierly, and as a result of his diligent labor, he has been awarded and now wears a diamond on his shoulder—a decoration that we all envy. Evidently this decoration attracts the fair sex of the neighboring towns, because J. E., has got the habit of heading for the mountains around Walla Walla every week-end. When he has persuaded some little girl to change her name to Westbury, we intend to borrow his diamond insignias and try our luck with the fair damsels.

name: White, William Albert
motto: If you do not want to be criticised, say nothing, do nothing, and be nothing.
clubs: Member Sophomore, Junior and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: "Doc" is a loyal Tiger, a good soldier, and a true friend. He hails from the "Power City." He has made a host of friends since coming to "Tigertown." Because of his big heart, his optimistic attitude, and his cheerful personality, he will be remembered long among his classmates. Doc decided to cast his lot with the chemists, and for the past four years he has been chasing molecules all over the Chemistry Building. Being a capable student, he generally "shoots" the profs for a "row of test tubes." All that we predict for you, Doc, is a bright future. May some fair damsel steer your ship of life into the port of happiness. Luck to you, Doc—a real honest-to-goodness gentleman.

name: Wray, James Quilla Jr.
motto: Life is not a goblet to be drained, but a measure to be filled.
clubs: Sophomore, Junior and Senior Dancing Clubs
Secretary and Treasurer Senior Dancing Club
First Class Gunner Howitzer, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: In "Jake" we have a man who does not aggressively push himself on anyone. He has always striven to bring honor to his Alma Mater, and to support anything that served to make the Class of '26 one of which we might be justly proud. He is a boy of sterling character, a friend on whom you may depend, and a gentleman in every sense of the word. On entering Clemson, Jake cast his lot among the chasers of electrons. He has the distinction of never having infuriated "Crip's" "Giant." Jake expects to sail the sea of electrical waves, and we predict a safe voyage for his ship "Success" with such a competent pilot at the helm.

name: Wright, John Brooks
motto: When duty and pleasure conflict, let your conscience be your guide.
clubs: First Class Machine Gunner, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: On a bright September morning in the fall of '22, there was a braying of asses, lowing of cattle, yelping of dogs, and everything seemed gloomy and dismal in Shelton when boarded the train for Clemson. was tired of chasing boll weevils at home, so he decided to help chase electrons; he became one of most able disciples. is in every sense of the word a man. Only those who know him best can fully appreciate the character and manliness of this youth. He has acquired a host of friends because of his genteel manner, cheerful countenance, and a willingness to lend a helping hand. Judging by his success during the four years at school, we feel safe in predicting that he will attain that success in life for which he is striving.

name: Zagora, Otto Frank
motto: Be true to your own conscience, as there is no better guide.
description: Otto entered Clemson as an artist, and departing leaves his mark indelibly engraved in the annals of musical accomplishment. He is possessed of a true artistic temperament which manifests itself in his precision and unflagging attention to details. Not only has Otto been a bolster in all of Clemson's musical organizations at one time or another, but he has delved fruitfully into our social life. Temperamental, precise, talented, and congenial, are four adjectives that aptly describe this engaging young violinist.

name: Byrd, Hubert Lee
motto: "What a man holds behind his teeth hurts neither himself nor anyone else."
clubs: First Class Machine Gunner, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Clemson's stock ran high in the fall of '22 when the town of North paid her debt to the State Institution. Hubert began his college career by taking Chemistry, but finding it very lonesome, he changed his course to Electricity. To know Hubert is to be acquainted with a good fellow and a true gentleman. A big heart, a generous disposition, and an eagerness to help his friends have won for him a host of friends while at Clemson. It would take several to express the esteem which we hold for Hubert. He is one of our most popular members, and the Class of '26 would be incomplete without him. Byrd has never claimed to be a ladies' man, but his frequent visits to the P. O. show that there is a certain young lady who is anxious for June to come. So here's wishing you success, Hubert, and may you some day electrify a cozy cabin for your own personal use.

name: Calvert, Julian Pinckney
motto: "One ship sails West, one ship sails East, By the self same wind that blows, It's the set of the sail, and not the gale, That tells which way your ship goes."
description: Just a few years ago Calvert was induced, after much persuasion, to board the "Tigertown Limited." Julian set his heart to be a Steinmetz; and he found it easy to see the oscillatory, vibratory, and rotary motion of the powerful molecules which so cheerfully pushed the throb of the mighty motors at the touch of his hand. In his life's study, and its associated student organizations, he has been satisfied with nothing but the highest and best. We find Calvert making excellent marks on his grades and excelling also in the Palmetto Literary Society. His excellent talent has meant much for this society. Calvert has a sterling character and has in every respect lent it to clean sportsmanship. We are proud to claim this loyal Tiger as our true friend and he has won a place in our hearts that shall not be forgotten. We know that he will succeed in his life's work—including the M. R. S. degree.

name: Carson, James Withrow
motto: Now is the time.
clubs: Sophomore, Junior and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: Besides making excellent grades during his four years at Clemson, he has won the esteem and friendship of everyone with whom he has come in contact. Jimmy is known for his sincerity, honesty, sound judgement, and gentlemanly manners. We are sure that these qualities will win for him a high place among men. Jimmy's favorite pastime is dancing, and no Hop would have been complete without Jimmy and his girl. During his last year in Clemson, Jimmy decided to join the ranks of the Bugle Corps and from the early hours of the morning until the late hours of night, one could hear the melodious notes of his bugle as it floated from Room 121. Jimmy is considered by all who know him as one of the neatest and most precise boys at Clemson. Whether at work or at play, these qualities are always foremost.

name: Carter, David Earl
motto: Aim right, start right, and keep pegging away.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Laurens County Club
description: To know David is to admire him. He who counts this man his friend is indeed fortunate, for no truer friend could be desired. His calm, serene personality has quite often been broken with well-said words of wit, intelligence, and frankness. These qualities have won for him many friends at Clemson. David is at all times an ardent supporter of college activities. After playing baseball with the scrubs for two years, the third year he attained his goal, a block "C". In his studies he is an earnest worker and always willing to do his part. With his determination and desire to succeed, we predict for him a great success in the work he pursues. Here's to you, David, may you have the best that the world affords.

name: Carpenter, Ernest Willaughby
motto: Trust not thyself; but, thy faults to know, make use of every friend and every foe.
description: To know Ern is to know that which, in the code of all his friends and associates, exemplifies a gentleman. Always courteous, friendly, and refined, he has established a reputation here as one who stands for those things which all the world admires; but not alone on this trait does his character depend. Ern is an earnest student, with more than the usual intelligence; a lover of all social life, gifted with the graces necessary; a lover of physical activities, with a well-developed body. During his stay at Clemson, he has entered whole-heartedly into all the various forms of student activities. He has contributed largely to all the various college publications and has distinguished himself with literary endeavors. It will be needless to predict for Ern a success in his future life, as all who know him know his capacities.

name: Cain, Ralph Henry
motto: Honesty, hard work, and fair play: these shall be the rules and guide of my life in all its phases.
clubs: President, Vice-President, Secretary and Treasurer, and Chaplain, Palmetto Literary Society
description: In Cain is embodied those traits which make a real man, and in his life there can be found no exception to the fair play, honesty, and hard work which he has chosen for the ruling lights of his life. There can be nothing but success for such a man, and we can only wish for him a life filled with the happiness of serving.
